mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that immediately triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, driving institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities and generating positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce a positive rank IC over 60-day forward returns in any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines during disinflationary periods signal deteriorating corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of observations when INDPRO declines during CPIAUCSL disinflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 3.0% triggers liquidity contraction that outweighs growth expectations, forcing immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of trading days where FEDFUNDS exceeds the 3.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment rates confirms labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yields a negative or zero rank IC for 60-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts expansion during periods of stable inflation signals sustainable consumer demand growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive predictive power when HOUST rises within the specified CPIAUCSL band over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production directly correlate with higher corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns exhibit a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with INDPRO month-over-month changes over any 2-year rolling period.
